title: Bomarea cordifolia
taxonomy: Bomarea cordifolia is a member of the family Alstroemeriaceae. This plant is a species of flowering plant, native to the Andes mountains in South America. The genus Bomarea is part of the order Asparagales, which includes several other plant species. Bomarea cordifolia is closely related to other plants in the family Alstroemeriaceae, which are also known as the gladiolus family. The classification of Bomarea cordifolia provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plant species.
uses: Bomarea cordifolia is a plant species with reported medicinal uses, including its application in treating respiratory issues and as an anti-inflammatory agent.
